vBRIEFInfo.description: Story vBRIEF for #1648 / #1659: remove task-runner coupling from the gated session ritual.

plan.title: fix(ritual): call gated session ritual entrypoints in-process

narratives.Description: Convert the #1648 session ritual fix from task namespace reconstruction to #1659's canonical in-process Python entrypoint pattern.
narratives.ImplementationPlan: Replace gated `task doctor` / `task verify:cache-fresh` re-entry with direct `doctor.cmd_doctor(...)` and `preflight_cache.main(...)` calls. Preserve the existing cache-fresh Taskfile wrapper behavior by passing `--allow-missing-bootstrap`.
narratives.UserStory: As a consumer project using a namespaced Deft Taskfile include, I want session startup gates to work without depending on how `task` exposes framework verbs.

narrative.Acceptance: `scripts/verify_session_ritual.py` no longer shells through `task` for doctor or cache freshness and records entrypoint-oriented command metadata.

items.title: Remove task-prefix coupling from session startup

narrative.Acceptance: `scripts/session_start.py` stops resolving or threading task prefixes for ritual state.

items.title: Retire namespace resolver from this PR

narrative.Acceptance: The PR does not use `scripts/task_namespace.py` as a gate mechanism; resolver-only tests are removed if the module has no remaining runtime use.
Focused verifier and session-start tests assert the in-process path and preserve cache-fresh bootstrap behavior.

references.title: Issue #1648: Gated session ritual invokes unprefixed task names
Issue #1659: Decouple framework runtime and guidance from the task runner
PR #1655: fix(ritual): resolve consumer task namespace
